subject: Fixing CSIT ETSI NFVO Failures on Master + Small Refactoring
commit_message:
Fixing CSIT ETSI NFVO Failures on Master + Small Refactoring

- Due to Camunda Engine changes we needed to update the Camunda Engine SQL file. We don't want to effect any other components, so replacing this just within our tests.
- Extracting image versions for MariaDB and NFVO containers.
- Updated Maven Repos to use HTTPS as required now.
- Refactored the WAIT script code in Setup.sh.

Change-Id: I8423e4009cea9b962a0eae64b3cbc7e267ac7366
Issue-ID: INT-1839
Signed-off-by: rope252 <gareth.roper@est.tech>

Patch Set 1:

What CSIT failures is this title referring to?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> What CSIT failures is this title referring to?

Hi Lasse, when pulling down Master CSIT tests and running the "integration-etsi-testing" we are seeing failures on our local system due to the NFVO NS LCM Container being unable to populate the MariaDB NFVO Tables.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > What CSIT failures is this title referring to?
> 
> Hi Lasse, when pulling down Master CSIT tests and running the "integration-etsi-testing" we are seeing failures on our local system due to the NFVO NS LCM Container being unable to populate the MariaDB NFVO Tables.

I can email you logs of the errors if you would like, it appears that the Flyway Dependency we use to populate the MariaDB Tables no longer supports MariaDB version 10.1 or older, shown by this error which appears in the SO-ETSI-NFVO-NS-LCM Container logs:

"org.flywaydb.core.internal.license.FlywayEditionUpgradeRequiredException: Flyway Pro Edition or MariaDB upgrade required: MariaDB 10.1 is no longer supported by Flyway Community Edition, but still supported by Flyway Pro Edition."

Upgrading the MariaDB Version then caused additional errors with the table creation for the NFVO DB within MariaDB. This was due to the Camunda Migration from 7.9 to 7.10, so in order to fix this we needed to edit the mariadb_engine_sql script, which creates the Camunda Database and tables. Happy to expand on this over email if easier.

Patch Set 1:

Why haven't we seen this error in Jenkins? Of course, it still needs to be fixed even if it's a problem only for local executions, but I want to understand this for academic reasons

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Why haven't we seen this error in Jenkins? Of course, it still needs to be fixed even if it's a problem only for local executions, but I want to understand this for academic reasons

We unfortunately are not sure why it failed locally but hasn't showed up on Jenkins. I initially thought it was just my environment however my team tried this on 3 separate machines, cleared all docker images/volumes, and all 3 saw the exact same issues. Speaking with my team, we think its possible the Jenkins jobs are using cached images this is only a theory however, I think some investigation into the specific Jenkins job would be needed.
Change has been successfully merged by Lasse Kaihlavirta
